title: Wire LATE_INNING_BLOCK into the active recommendation-to-entry path
subsystem: entry gates / inning context / runtime config enforcement
depends_on: LATE_INNING_BLOCK_WIRING_VERIFY_001 (DONE — inert-config confirmed). This task is the current active task. CONFIG_HASH_INPUTS_FIX_001 and STARTUP_PROOF_BLOCK_001 are queued behind it.

context.verify_summary: LATE_INNING_BLOCK_WIRING_VERIFY_001 confirmed that LATE_INNING_BLOCK=7 exists in .env and inning context exists on the live recommendation object, but the config value is not consumed anywhere in the active recommendation-to-entry path. It is not read in recommendation_api.py, model_bridge.py, or risk.py. bot_core.py does not pass inning context into check_entry_gates(). The block is inert — this is a proven config-enforcement gap, not a theory.
context.inning_data_status: Already present on the recommendation/signal object. The missing link is not data acquisition — it is wiring: no active gate consumes inning + LATE_INNING_BLOCK together.
context.config_value: LATE_INNING_BLOCK=7 in .env — block new entries from inning 7 onward.

objective: Wire a single late-inning entry block into the narrowest active gate surface in the live path so that LATE_INNING_BLOCK=7 actually rejects new entries from inning 7 onward. This must use the existing configured value and existing inning context — no new data acquisition needed.
conditional_allowed_files: C:\Users\johnny\Desktop\sports_bot_v2\.env — read-only unless the worker proves a tiny plumbing addition (e.g., adding LATE_INNING_BLOCK to the env-var block near top of risk.py) is strictly necessary. .env content must not be modified unless the value is missing or incorrect.
forbidden_files: Any file not listed in allowed_files or conditional_allowed_files
forbidden_actions: Edit recommendation_api.py or model_bridge.py — the fix must land at the gate surface, not the model layer
Add new inning-data acquisition paths — inning is already on the signal object
Widen into broader model redesign or strategy changes
Mix this task with CONFIG_HASH_INPUTS_FIX_001 or STARTUP_PROOF_BLOCK_001 work
Activate this task while CONFIG_HASH_INPUTS_FIX_001 or STARTUP_PROOF_BLOCK_001 is ACTIVE (file lock)

implementation_guidance.preferred_surface: The narrowest active gate surface with access to both inning context and the entry decision. Two clean options — worker must pick one and justify:
implementation_guidance.option_a_bot_core: In bot_core.py, add an explicit pre-gate check immediately before the check_entry_gates() call. Read signal.inning (or equivalent field name on the Signal object). If inning is not None and inning >= LATE_INNING_BLOCK (read from os.getenv at module level or imported from risk), reject with a log line and continue. This option keeps risk.py signature unchanged.
implementation_guidance.option_b_risk_py: In risk.py check_entry_gates(), add an optional `inning` parameter (default None) and a LATE_INNING_BLOCK read at module level (alongside other env reads). If inning is not None and inning >= LATE_INNING_BLOCK, return False with reason 'late_inning_block'. Then update the bot_core.py call site to pass inning=getattr(signal, 'inning', None). This option keeps gate logic consolidated in risk.py.
implementation_guidance.field_name_to_verify: Worker must confirm the exact field name for inning on the Signal/intent object at the bot_core.py call site (verify via a grep of core/types.py or the signal unpacking in bot_core.py).
implementation_guidance.none_handling: If inning is None (pre-game or data missing), do NOT block. The gate must be a no-op when inning data is unavailable.
implementation_guidance.log_format: Gate reject log should be consistent with existing BRIDGE GATE REJECT format, e.g.: BRIDGE GATE REJECT [late_inning_block] slug=<slug> inning=<N>>=<threshold>

required_work: Confirm the exact field name for inning on the Signal/intent object at the bot_core.py call site (check core/types.py and the signal variable in the bridge loop)
Confirm LATE_INNING_BLOCK env var is not already read in risk.py at module level (likely absent — add if needed)
Choose and justify Option A (pre-gate in bot_core.py) or Option B (risk.py gate parameter) based on which is cleaner for this codebase
Implement the chosen option with minimal diff — no unrelated changes
Add a BRIDGE GATE REJECT log line so the block is observable in the log
Run python -m py_compile on every changed Python file
State whether a process restart is required
acceptance_criteria: New entries at or after inning 7 are blocked and logged with a BRIDGE GATE REJECT [late_inning_block] line
Entries with inning=None (pre-game, missing data) are NOT blocked
LATE_INNING_BLOCK=7 config value is consumed from os.environ — not hardcoded
No files outside allowed scope are modified
python -m py_compile core/risk.py passes if risk.py is modified
Diff is narrow: only the gate surface and call site are changed
verification_required: Show the exact lines changed in each file
Show the log format of the new BRIDGE GATE REJECT message
Confirm the field name used for inning on the signal object
Confirm which option was implemented (A or B) and why
Run py_compile on all changed files and report PASS/FAIL
